summary: Public API endpoints must accept only the smallest required request shape and bound untrusted input before database or billing side effects.
invariants: Billing API routes must verify Appwrite JWTs at the route boundary and reject request bodies because billing plan, customer, and redirect choices are server-owned.
Authorization headers must be length-bounded and syntactically safe before being forwarded to Appwrite verification.
Verified Appwrite identity fields must be normalized and length-bounded before SQL writes or Stripe metadata use.
Stripe webhook routes must bound raw body size, verify Stripe-Signature before parsing JSON, and reject malformed event envelopes.
Checkout redirects must be HTTPS Stripe-hosted URLs before being returned by the server or followed by the client.
Database calls must continue to use parameterized queries instead of concatenating user-controlled SQL.
